ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: The ordinary Shadcn catalog has no standalone hydration-neutral ClientOnly primitive; consumers commonly write a mounted-state fragment themselves.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: ClientOnly requires structurally valid server and first-hydration fallback content, adds no wrapper, and offers a Strict-Mode-safe one-shot client-ready handshake.

visualSignature.summary: The nonvisual fragment intentionally adds no chrome; its fallback and ready states are shown inside the shared literal-white, ink-structured foundation workbench.

optionalEnhancements.summary: A consumer can request one post-mount readiness callback without adding markup or announcement behavior.

disabledBehavior.ui: No readiness counter or component-owned DOM is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Fallback replacement still occurs after mount, but no readiness integration runs.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No role, live region, focus movement, or accessibility-tree node is added.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Automated SSR structure and browser axe checks pass; no-JavaScript, focus replacement, screen-reader, and layout-shift review remain unrecorded.

interactionEvidence.summary: Browser evidence verifies mounted replacement and a single readiness callback; packed and lifecycle matrices remain pending.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: No-JavaScript, focus-order, layout-stability, and screen-reader fallback evidence is not yet recorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn documents RTL installation and a DirectionProvider, with install-time logical-style transforms; the baseline does not document a native/context boundary with optional bidi isolation and physical-side resolution.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora changes React direction context and native dir atomically, carries context through portals, resolves logical sides deterministically, and can isolate a bidi subtree explicitly.

visualSignature.summary: Direction remains semantically and visually neutral while the shared workbench uses logical ink rails and literal Canvas tokens in both reading directions.

optionalEnhancements.summary: The boundary can isolate its bidi ordering from surrounding content without changing native or React semantics.

disabledBehavior.ui: No visual surface or isolation marker is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: unicode-bidi isolation is absent; native dir and React context remain synchronized.
disabledBehavior.events: No event is registered or emitted in either mode.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No role, name, focus order, or accessibility-tree output is added.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Native dir/context, mixed-direction bdi content, RTL portal context, and axe are automated; manual RTL reading order, keyboard, screen-reader, and zoom evidence remains.

interactionEvidence.summary: Nested context and isolation opt-out are tested, but the full RTL keyboard and browser matrix is not recorded.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: RTL reading order, keyboard, mixed-direction screen-reader, and 400 percent zoom evidence remains unrecorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn controls such as Button include component-local focus-visible styling, but the catalog does not expose a standalone semantic-preserving focus treatment with selectable contrast geometry.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: FocusRing supplies one coherent Deep Violet and Canvas two-cue indicator across arbitrary native children, with strong geometry and an explicit forced-colors mapping.

visualSignature.summary: A Deep Violet outer line separated by a Canvas seam stays distinct from green selected/pressed states, becomes wider in strong mode, and maps to system Highlight.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Consumers can increase both seam and outline geometry for unpredictable adjacent surfaces.

disabledBehavior.ui: The wider strong geometry is absent and standard focus geometry remains.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Focus-visible still receives the immediate shared indicator without strong-mode sizing.
disabledBehavior.events: No event or motion is added by either contrast mode.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No role or accessible output changes; forced-colors continues to use system Highlight.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Keyboard focus, standard/strong computed geometry, narrow layout, forced colors, and axe are automated; manual focus area/contrast and 400 percent zoom review remains.

interactionEvidence.summary: Chromium keyboard modality and both geometry modes are tested; the required cross-browser modality matrix remains incomplete.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Measured indicator area, adjacent-color contrast, zoom, and assistive-technology review is not recorded across all official themes.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn Dialog provides inert modal behavior within that component, but it does not document a shared cross-overlay registration, Escape-order, and environment-ownership manager.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: LayerManager unifies visual and Escape order across overlay types while allowing exactly one named engine to own inerting and scroll prevention.

visualSignature.summary: The manager adds no panel chrome and maps registration order to the shared semantic modal layer, preserving the workbench's structural order rather than arbitrary z-index experiments.

optionalEnhancements.summary: A registered layer can opt into native modal environment ownership or remain stack-only when another behavior engine owns those effects.

disabledBehavior.ui: No manager-owned modal surface is added in either mode.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Application inerting, body mutation, and scroll locking are absent while ordinary stack order remains.
disabledBehavior.events: Environment effects and their cleanup do not run; Escape routing remains only for registered dismissible layers.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No inert accessibility-tree suppression, role, label, focus movement, or live region is added by the manager.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Automated inert opt-in/opt-out, top-only Escape, restoration, and axe checks pass; complete Risk Class 3 desktop/mobile AT, speech, switch, and focus integration remains.

interactionEvidence.summary: Deterministic model and Chromium stack tests cover mutable registration and blocked Escape; scroll restoration, Strict Mode, touch scroll, and scale campaigns remain.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Desktop/mobile AT, speech, switch, focus restoration integration, RTL, zoom, forced-colors, and touch-scroll evidence is incomplete.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn overlay components portal internally, but the public catalog does not provide a standalone portal that bridges locale, native direction, density, deterministic fallback, and inline opt-out.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Portal repeats native locale, direction, and density at the target and preserves deterministic server/first-hydration output instead of relying on React context alone.

visualSignature.summary: The display-contents bridge adds no decorative wrapper; portaled content retains the shared literal-white workbench context and logical ink structure.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Consumers can provide accurate server and first-hydration portal content independently of the mounted portal.

api.enableWhen: fallback is a non-null React node.

disabledBehavior.ui: No temporary server or first-hydration portal content is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The mounted portal still targets the explicit, provider, or body container.
disabledBehavior.events: No fallback event or target interaction occurs before mount.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No temporary status, role, focus target, or accessibility-tree node is emitted.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: SSR fallback opt-out, inherited native context, list validity, and browser axe are tested; cross-root relationships, focus order, mobile AT, and zoom remain manual blockers.

interactionEvidence.summary: Mounted custom-target behavior and inline SSR mode are tested; nested portals and complete browser focus-order evidence remain.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Desktop/mobile AT relationships, cross-root focus order, RTL, and zoom evidence is not recorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn overlays commonly rely on their underlying primitive and CSS animation lifecycle; no standalone deadline-aware Presence utility is documented in the component catalog.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Presence keeps server content visible, ignores premature multi-property end events, guarantees a deadline, cancels interrupted exits, and follows provider motion policy.

visualSignature.summary: Only lifecycle-clarifying opacity motion is applied, using shared overlay timing and the semantic reduced-duration fallback.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Consumers can opt visible server content into a first-client entering lifecycle without changing the default no-JavaScript visibility contract.

disabledBehavior.ui: Present content starts fully visible instead of displaying an entering state.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The initial entering lifecycle and initial motion are absent; later exit/interruption behavior remains.
disabledBehavior.events: No initial transition or animation completion is awaited or emitted.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: Content is immediately present with unchanged semantics and no announcement or focus movement.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Server visibility, absent output, reduced-motion removal, and axe are automated; focused-content removal and desktop/mobile AT evidence remain manual.

interactionEvidence.summary: State transitions, deadlines, interrupted exit, and reduced motion are automated; Strict Mode, no-JavaScript, and complete browser lifecycle campaigns remain.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Focused-content removal, desktop/mobile AT, no-JavaScript, and manual reduced-motion evidence is incomplete.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn documents configuration and framework/provider compositions separately; the component catalog does not expose one provider for deterministic locale, direction, time zone, density, motion, messages, and portal targeting.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: MergoraProvider synchronizes native locale/direction with deterministic translation, density, reduced-motion, time-zone, and portal policies and scopes every override to a subtree.

visualSignature.summary: Provider maps comfortable, compact, and 48px touch targets plus normal/reduced motion to shared semantic variables without one-off component styling.

optionalEnhancements.summary: The provider can merge its native and Mergora context attributes into one concrete child when a wrapper would break layout.

disabledBehavior.ui: A neutral provider div remains instead of wrapper-free child composition.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Attribute merging into the child is absent; all context values and subtree token policies remain.
disabledBehavior.events: No event handlers are inferred or emitted in either mode.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No role, name, focus behavior, or live-region output is inferred; native lang and dir remain on the provider div.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Nested locale/direction, native attributes, portal context, density, reduced motion, and axe are automated; screen-reader locale, RTL, forced-colors, and official-locale review remains.

interactionEvidence.summary: Nested inheritance, message merge, wrapper-free rendering, density, and motion variables are tested; full hydration and portal browser matrices remain.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Screen-reader locale, RTL, forced-colors, reduced-motion, and official-locale manual evidence remains incomplete.
Semantic Sync lifecycle evidence remains pending.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn Button documents asChild polymorphism, ordinarily backed by Slot-style cloning, but does not publish Mergora's full child-authoritative merge and cancellation contract as a component API.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Slot deterministically preserves child semantics and names while composing refs, context attributes, classes, styles, and child-first cancellable events.

visualSignature.summary: Slot introduces no wrapper or one-off style; the composed native child receives the shared workbench control and focus treatment.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Consumers can add a Slot-owned event layer that runs after the child's handler and respects child preventDefault cancellation.

api.enableWhen: A Slot-owned event handler is supplied.

disabledBehavior.ui: No orchestration status UI is rendered by the story and Slot itself never adds UI.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Only the child's native behavior and handler run.
disabledBehavior.events: No Slot-owned listener or callback is composed or emitted.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: The child's role, name, focus behavior, and accessibility tree remain unchanged.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: SSR semantics/name precedence, invalid children, native button keyboard behavior, and axe are automated; desktop screen-reader name and manual keyboard replacement review remains.

interactionEvidence.summary: Optional event orchestration and child-native activation are browser-tested; complete ref, nested event cancellation, and packed composition campaigns remain.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Manual keyboard-native-semantics and desktop screen-reader accessible-name review remains.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: The ordinary Shadcn component catalog has visible toast/status compositions but no standalone localized, paced, dual-priority screen-reader announcement queue.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: ScreenReaderAnnouncer separates polite and assertive FIFO queues, paces delivery, deduplicates by semantic key, localizes descriptors, and reuses the nearest provider.

visualSignature.summary: The live regions remain visually absent and avoid decorative status UI; persistent actions stay in the literal-white, ink-structured foundation workbench.

optionalEnhancements.summary: A consumer can bypass repeat suppression for one genuinely new user-relevant occurrence without changing queue priority or live-region roles.

api.enableWhen: dedupe is false for an announce call.

disabledBehavior.ui: The intentional-repeat action is absent from the basic story; the component never adds visible UI.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Repeated semantic keys inside the dedupe window are suppressed.
disabledBehavior.events: No duplicate announcement record is queued or delivered.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No repeated live-region text or additional accessibility announcement is emitted.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Live-region anatomy, provider opt-out, queue IDs, dedupe/repeat behavior, clear, and axe are automated; real desktop/mobile AT timing and interruption/noise evidence remains.

interactionEvidence.summary: FIFO, priority, dedupe, intentional repeat, clear, and nested-provider reuse are automated; assistive-technology timing and high-volume campaigns remain.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Desktop/mobile AT polite/assertive timing, interruption, announcement-noise, and RTL evidence remains unrecorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn examples use visually hidden text and icon-button names as local composition details, but the catalog does not document a polymorphic reveal-on-focus utility with logical placement and forced-colors treatment.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: VisuallyHidden keeps content nameable with valid element-specific props and can become a logical-start, literal Canvas skip-link surface with forced-colors-safe focus.

visualSignature.summary: The revealed surface uses literal Canvas, an ink border, restrained shared corners, logical-start placement, and the Deep Violet/system Highlight focus treatment.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Focusable hidden content can opt into a readable, logical-start Mergora surface only for keyboard focus-visible.

api.enableWhen: revealOnFocus is true on a natively focusable element.

disabledBehavior.ui: The fixed Canvas skip-link surface and ink border are absent.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Focus does not reveal the hidden geometry; the basic story avoids a focusable permanently hidden link.
disabledBehavior.events: No event listener, callback, or motion is added.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: The content remains in the accessibility tree, but no extra focusable skip-link node or focus-reveal announcement is created by the story.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Type-safe elements, accessibility-tree content, focus reveal, narrow layout, forced colors, RTL logical placement, and axe are automated; manual desktop screen-reader, text spacing, and 400 percent zoom remains.

interactionEvidence.summary: Keyboard reveal and opt-out are Chromium-tested; complete browser, zoom, and text-spacing interaction review remains.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Desktop screen-reader, 400 percent zoom, text-spacing, and manual forced-colors evidence remains unrecorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.
